svg.text: Ceph BlueStore Internals: queue_transactions → (deferred / direct) → RocksDB on BlueFS → Allocator → raw block
Section 1 — entry: BlueStore::queue_transactions splits the op into deferred (small) vs direct (large) write paths
aio_write data direct to block dev
to RocksDB; commit after both ack
- direct allocate + write costs head seek
- WAL is sequential append, much cheaper
- coalesce many small writes per WAL flush
- doubling payload through WAL is waste
- direct allocate, single write, only meta in WAL
Section 2 — BlueStore (object engine) + RocksDB (k/v metadata) + BlueFS (mini FS just for RocksDB SST + WAL files)
- BlueStore has no FS, only raw block
- BlueFS is ′just enough′ FS for RocksDB
- replays its own log on mount
all files end up as extents on block device
- if block.db fills, RocksDB SST spills
- BlueFS log replays on OSD start
Section 3 — Allocator (Bitmap / AVL) + raw block device with shared regions for BlueStore object data and BlueFS
- replayed from BlueFS log on start
raw block device (e.g. /dev/sdb, opened O_DIRECT, no kernel FS)
free-space tracked by Allocator covers both BlueFS area and BlueStore data area on the same block device
optional: separate block.db / block.wal device (NVMe SSD)
if block.db missing → SST & WAL fall back onto main block device (single-device OSD)
Source: ceph v19.2.3 src/os/bluestore/{BlueStore, BlueFS, Allocator}.{h,cc}
